publicV1.reason: The shared @cap/contracts module the Public V1 surface consumes gains the admin-console-only UpdateSandboxEnvironmentParameters schemas; no /v1 operation, request/response schema, status code, or error body is added or altered, and the public-surface parity suite passes with zero drift.

mcp.reason: The same shared contracts module change is visible to the MCP surface's package dependency, but no MCP tool, argument, result shape, registration, or transport behavior changes; tools/list and adapter parity evidence is unchanged.

openapi.reason: The OpenAPI document projects only registry operations; the new admin-console schemas are not registered, so the generated document is byte-identical and only the shared contracts source module changed.

apiPlayground.reason: The API Playground catalogs Public V1 operations only; it gains no entry because the parameter-edit endpoint is admin-console scoped, and only the shared contracts source module it transitively consumes changed.

internalOnly.reason: Adds an admin-gated console API endpoint to replace the image parameter set of a registered sandbox environment, extends the shared contracts package with the request schema, and adds an edit dialog to the Image Management web console; secret values stay write-only through the existing settings/secret-storage encryption path.

protocolDifferences.detail: Each MCP tool call is already a distinct invocation; HTTP retry deduplication via Idempotency-Key remains REST-only.
Preserve the historical MCP text handle while structuredContent remains the canonical task.
Explain asynchronous polling and the HTTP-only idempotency header without claiming MCP accepts that header.
REST keeps its dedicated task-create throttle while MCP remains subject to the MCP transport limiter.
REST applies a dedicated per-principal catalog throttle while MCP remains subject to the MCP transport limiter; both retain the shared catalog-service capacity controls.

The MCP SDK high-level registrar accepts only a root object output schema; derive its advertised fields from the canonical SessionHistory discriminated union while validating every structured result against that canonical union.
MCP tools use request/response transport; lifecycle SSE is REST-only.
REST returns 204 without a body; MCP requires a structured tool result.
